CREATE TABLE sales (product_id INT, quantity INT, revenue FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE employees (employee_id INT, department_id INT, salary FLOAT);
SELECT max_by(employee_id, salary, 1) FROM employees WHERE department_id = 1;
SELECT max_by(product_id, quantity, 3) FROM sales ORDER BY revenue DESC;
CREATE TABLE department (department_id INT, name VARCHAR);
SELECT max_by(e.employee_id, e.salary, 1) FROM employees e JOIN department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Sales';
SELECT max_by(A.product_id, A.revenue, 2) FROM (SELECT product_id, revenue FROM sales WHERE quantity > 100) AS A;
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id INT, product_id INT, order_date DATE);
SELECT max_by(o.order_id, o.order_date, 2) FROM orders o JOIN sales s ON o.product_id = s.product_id WHERE s.revenue > 1000;
CREATE TABLE performance (id INT, category VARCHAR, score FLOAT);
SELECT max_by(p.id, p.score, 2) FROM performance p GROUP BY p.category HAVING AVG(p.score) > 75;
